name: PandaPick closed-loop force-regulated manipulation cell
robot: Franka Emika Panda (7-DOF arm + parallel gripper), MjSpec-composed scene
controller: CLOSED-LOOP contact-force-regulated grasp: P-control on mj_contactForce (EMA-filtered) drives the gripper actuator to a calibrated setpoint, then secures for transport; layered on resolved-rate (damped-least-squares Jacobian) IK on mj_jacSite with interpolated joint-target trajectories
role: scripted expert / demonstration generator (deterministic, by design) with a genuine inner force feedback loop - NOT a learned/RL policy; no RL is claimed

loop.sensor: mj_contactForce summed over fingertip<->cube contacts (fingertip geoms resolved by body, not name); NORMAL component regulates the grasp, TANGENTIAL (shear) component reads payload weight (haptic mass ID, r=1.0)
loop.law: dctrl = -clip(kp * (target_N - force_ema), -dmax, dmax); terminates on |error| < 0.3 N sustained, not a fixed step count
loop.auditable: blinding the force sensor changes the converged grip (sensored ~1.36 N vs sensor-cut ~1.74 N) - see audit.py

tasks: fragile (gentle-carry under a 1.5 N force budget)
haptic payload identification (object mass from fingertip shear force)
integrity: cubes are freejoint bodies; NO teleport, NO weld, NO equality shortcut. Every number is read live from MuJoCo (mj_forward/qpos/mj_jacSite/mj_contactForce) and cross-checked by validate_submission.py (README == benchmark.json) and audit.py (loop is real). 2-finger parallel-jaw force control (~2 N regime); no dexterity / 5-finger claims. Sim-only; no real-robot claims.
compute: CPU only, no GPU; one command (python run.py)
